programmes.show_description: Nathania's writing a dissertation about marriage as a form of punishment in fairy tales, but is songwriter Dan - who hasn't had a girlfriend since school - her Prince Charming?
Mula, who was stood up on her last date, is hoping round two will be better. Her blind date is another returner, funny man AJ, who compares women to a bucket of KFC.
Britain's top armchair critics watch The Celebrity Traitors, Trigger Point, Michael McIntyre's The Wheel, Bake Off, Love Is Blind, DIY SOS and BBC News (clocks).
The Celebrity Traitors is on this time. There's Game of Wool: Britain's Best Knitter. And Trigger Point, Hunted, Squid Game: The Challenge and Hamza's Hidden Wild Isles.
Davide: In Boston, Gordon Ramsay visits Davide, an Italian restaurant that's been run into the ground by brothers Frank and Anthony. Can they learn to trust each other again?
Café Kingston: In California, Gordon visits Jamaican restaurant Café Kingston. He meets micromanaging owner Dr Una Morris, who's also a radiotherapist.
A new cellmate spells danger for Lucas, forcing Dillon to take desperate measures. Theresa pushes Mercedes to give Warren another chance. Diane and Tony lie to protect their loved ones.
Yes-Woman: To be less negative, Claire rebrands herself as the woman who says 'yes', and inadvertently approves of Luke's new relationship with a colleague.
Can't Elope: Haley and Dylan's plan to quietly elope before the babies are born does not go as hoped. Jay tries to get his new business off the ground by courting an Insta celeb.
(2020) Family action comedy. A CIA agent (Dave Bautista) gets the run-around from a nine-year-old girl when he's sent to keep an eye on her family. With Chloe Coleman.
Maisie Adam and Sam Campbell talk about the new series of Last One Laughing. Andi Osho chats about her new book. Plus: Lucinda Light, Glenn Tilbrook and music from Don Broco.
A short, cheeky clip from Rude Tube's naughty little sister Rude(ish) Tube, featuring sheep.
Brand new series: One couple are smitten on honeymoon. Another are very much not. And two new couples get hitched, including flashy English groom Danny.
Brand new series: The experiment intensifies. Gia considers taking a big step after years of celibacy, while tensions explode in Fiji for Bec and Danny.
(2017) Fantasy adventure sequel with Dwayne Johnson, Karen Gillan, Kevin Hart and Jack Black. Four teenagers are sucked into the Jumanji video game to complete a dangerous quest.
Brand new series: Eleven Aussie celebs are paired up with close ones in a thrilling, globe-trotting race to win money for charity. First stop: the busy streets of New Delhi.
Lisa's Belly: Bart and Lisa have to take steroids after getting an infection at a waterpark - and the new drugs have some alarming side effects, particularly for Lisa.
Portrait of a Lackey on Fire: Smithers tires of Mr Burns' indifference and finds solace in the arms of affectionate fashion designer Michael de Graaf.
(2005) Violent action-thriller starring Jason Statham as the tough courier Frank Martin, who's hired to guard a government drugs czar's son. But his mission soon lurches towards disaster.
The first episode of the daring dating show. Music producer Aina wants to find someone adventurous. Designer Mal, who has dated men and women, has all options laid bare before her.
On the box this time: I'm a Celebrity... South Africa, Married at First Sight Australia, Obsession, Britain's Got Talent, ITV News (government phone alarm) and more.
The Goggleboxers have been watching I'm a Celebrity... South Africa, Citadel, The 1% Club, Celebrity Hunted, Britain's Got Talent, Reunion Hotel and Canal Boat Diaries.
Ann-Marie visits the First Dates restaurant on her 50th birthday. She's survived four bouts of cancer and believes time is precious. Will she want to spend it with her date Carl?
The first episode of the daring dating show. Music producer Aina wants to find someone adventurous. Designer Mal, who has dated men and women, has all options laid bare before her.
Commencement: Cam finally gets a chance to shine when he's asked to preside over the high school graduation ceremony at the last minute, while Jay gives the commencement address.
A Year of Birthdays: As the birth of Haley and Dylan's twins approaches, the family look back at how each of them celebrated their birthdays this past year.
A new cellmate spells danger for Lucas, forcing Dillon to take desperate measures. Theresa pushes Mercedes to give Warren another chance. Diane and Tony lie to protect their loved ones.
Theresa pushes Mercedes to give Warren another chance. A phone call makes Dillon concerned about Lucas's wellbeing.
A new cellmate spells danger for Lucas, forcing Dillon to take desperate measures. Diane and Tony continue to lie to protect their loved ones. A resident's romantic hopes end in betrayal.
A short, cheeky clip from Rude Tube's naughty little sister, Rude(ish) Tube, featuring sports of the lawnmower, motorbike and bicycle variety.
Capri: In Eagle Rock, California, Gordon helps Capri, a restaurant in a rut that's owned by identical twins Jim and Jeff and is closed more often than it's open.
There Can Be Only One Highlander Club: Adam and Johnny Atkins compete to become head of the Highlander Club at school, but Beverly would prefer them to bond.
Our Perfect Strangers: Erica, Barry and Adam aren't happy when their distant relative Gleb visits from Russia, until they realise that he can make their lives easier.
Dead on a Rival: Mitchell learns a valuable life lesson when Murray shows up unexpectedly. And the Dunphys' old neighbour Kenneth, who's now a tech titan, visits.
Paris: The family head to Paris so that Jay can accept a lifetime achievement award for his work in the closet industry. But his old nemesis, Earl Chambers, haunts him.
Brand new series: The experiment intensifies. Gia considers taking a big step after years of celibacy, while tensions explode in Fiji for Bec and Danny.
Tory Jessie is far from conservative when it comes to talking about sex and politics. How will she get on with journalist Josh? Ex-roadie Winston meets glam gran Donna.
Gordon Ramsay revisits three restaurants - Mojito, The Junction (formerly Flamangos) and Bazzini - to check in with the owners. Is he pleased with their progress?
The Mook, the Chef, the Wife and Her Homer: Homer becomes the boss of Fat Tony's mob when Tony's son decides to become a chef rather than go into the family business.
Jazzy and the Pussycats: Bart takes up playing the drums, upstages Lisa and joins a top jazz band. So Lisa turns her back on music and starts rescuing animals instead.
Coal Digger: The family are invited to Jay and Gloria's for a barbecue and football, but an incident at school between Manny and Luke makes things awkward for Gloria and Claire.
Run for Your Wife: On the kids' first day back at school, Phil oversteps when he misreads how Claire's coping with an empty nest.
Brand new series: The experiment intensifies. Gia considers taking a big step after years of celibacy, while tensions explode in Fiji for Bec and Danny.
Week two of the trip kicks off in Praia D'El Rey in Portugal, where the wheels come off as the celebs race go-karts and put their twist on synchronised swimming.
Revenge Is a Dish Best Served Three Times: Homer's misguided revenge on Cletus combined with an untimely car breakdown leaves the Simpsons telling tales of revenge gone wrong.
Little Big Girl: Lisa invents fragments of her family's history to impress her classmates, but feels guilty about lying. Bart thinks he's got a girl pregnant by kissing her.
Lucas has a tough decision to make in order to protect himself from a dangerous cellmate. Elsewhere, Warren is determined to uncover the truth from someone, no matter the cost.
Australia - Brand new series: The final weddings arrive, including a MAFS Aus first: Julia walks down the aisle not knowing whether she'll be marrying a man or a woman!
Brand new series: The Chelsea set head for Scotland, hoping for a Highland fling, but a blast from Sam Prince's past sends shockwaves. And things are frosty for Livvy and Chuggs.
Brand new series - FB to the T: Twisted mystery romcom with Stephanie Hsu. More of Ruby's exes die. And she makes a big mistake at an ex's wedding. (Ep2/8)
The Goggleboxers have been watching: The Coronation - and The Coronation Concert - First Dates, The 1% Club, The Greatest Auction, Made in Chelsea and BBC News (local elections).
Ann-Marie visits the First Dates restaurant on her 50th birthday. She's survived four bouts of cancer and believes time is precious. Will she want to spend it with her date Carl?
